title: hieracium pyrenaicum subsp. ibericum
intro: Hieracium pyrenaicum subsp. ibericum, commonly known as the Pyrenean hawkweed, is a plant species in the dandelion-like genus Hieracium. It is a flowering plant native to the Iberian Peninsula, used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, particularly in treating digestive issues and wounds. The plant's leaves and roots contain compounds with anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which can help to soothe digestive issues, promote wound healing, and exhibit antioxidant activity. Hieracium pyrenaicum subsp. ibericum can be prepared as a tea, tincture, or dried herb, and is often used in herbal remedies for its health benefits.
